backend/main.mo: Main Motoko actor for the APA 9-Ball Scorekeeper backend, handling ownership, access control, invite-only mode, user approval, user profiles, and match history storage.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report confirming that a 'Debug' text link was added to the AppLayout footer to toggle the DebugPanel component open and closed.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: Auto-generated JSON summary file describing the purpose and key behaviors of each frontend source file in the project.
frontend/docs/v48-modecard-resume-button: Documentation for a UI-only change in version 48 that reorders ModeCard buttons so 'Start New Game' appears before 'Resume Game'.
frontend/index.css: Global Tailwind CSS stylesheet defining light and dark mode CSS custom properties for the app's design token system.
frontend/index.html: Root HTML entry point for the Vite/React frontend, mounting the app into a div with id 'root'.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Main React application component that sets up TanStack Router with all app routes, wraps everything in ThemeProvider and AuthGate, and provides a shared error fallback UI.
frontend/src/components/accepting-gifts/: Table component displaying win/loss records grouped by starting ball count (2–7) for the Accepting Gifts game mode.
frontend/src/components/apa/ApaBallButto: Circular button component representing a pool ball in APA scoring, supporting unscored, playerA, playerB, and dead states with appropriate styling and accessibility labels.
frontend/src/components/apa/apaBallStyle: Provides shared utility functions for APA 9-Ball rack scoring, including ball point value calculation and rack totals computation with one-away rule logic for the 9-ball.
frontend/src/components/apa/ApaRackScori: React component for scoring a single rack in an APA 9-ball match, managing ball states (unscored/playerA/playerB/dead), defensive shots, innings flow, and emitting rack completion data when all balls are assigned.
frontend/src/components/apa/ApaResultsSu: Displays a post-match summary card showing each player's skill level, points needed/earned, defensive shots, innings, PPI, and the team match point outcome with winner highlighted.
frontend/src/components/apa/MatchupAnaly: A dropdown button that lists unique opponents from official APA matches and calls a callback when one is selected; renders as disabled if no opponents exist.
Extended matchup analysis dropdown that filters matches by opponent (with an optional practice-match toggle), sorts them chronologically, and renders a MatchupAnalysisPanel for the selected opponent.
Panel component that aggregates official and practice APA matches against a specific opponent, computes win/loss outcomes and PPI for each, and displays head-to-head statistics.
frontend/src/components/apa/RealApaMatch: Form component for creating or editing an official APA match log entry, handling skill level selection, score/innings/defensive-shot inputs with validation, and saving via mutation hooks.
frontend/src/components/apa/useApaInning: React hook that manages the innings counter and active player turn state for APA 9-Ball matches, incrementing innings when the bottom player (lag loser) ends their turn.
frontend/src/components/auth/AuthGate.ts: Authentication gate component that blocks rendering of children until the user is logged in via Internet Identity and passes invite-only access control checks.
frontend/src/components/auth/LoginButton: Button component that handles Internet Identity login and logout, clearing the React Query cache on logout.
frontend/src/components/auth/ProfileSetu: Dialog component that prompts newly authenticated users to create a profile (name and APA skill level) and allows existing users to edit their profile.
frontend/src/components/debug/DebugPanel: A slide-out debug panel that runs live canister diagnostics by calling backend methods (getOwner, getCallerPrincipal, isCallerAdmin, etc.) and displaying their raw results.
frontend/src/components/history/MatchHis: Displays a paginated table of past matches with selectable rows, bulk delete functionality, and columns for date, match results narrative, PPI, and aPPI.
frontend/src/components/layout/AppLayout: Main application layout component with a sticky header containing navigation buttons, authentication state handling, admin controls, and a claim-ownership flow using Internet Identity.
frontend/src/components/matches/DeleteMa: Button component that triggers a confirmation dialog before permanently deleting a match, then navigates to history or calls an optional callback on success.
frontend/src/components/matches/EndMatch: Reusable alert dialog component for confirming match termination, with configurable title, description, confirm text, and pending/disabled states.
frontend/src/components/matches/MatchSum: Card component that displays a brief summary of a match including mode pill, formatted date, narrative details, and a link to the full match view.
frontend/src/components/ModePill.tsx: Small presentational component that renders a styled pill/badge displaying a game mode name, used in match history views.
frontend/src/components/navigation/ModeC: Navigation card for a game mode that shows a start button and optionally a resume button when an in-progress session is detected in local storage.
frontend/src/components/players/ApaAggre: Renders line charts for APA performance trends (PPI, aPPI, points, defensive shots) over time using Recharts, with custom dot shapes and a unified timestamp-keyed dataset.
frontend/src/components/straight-shot/St: Bar chart component that visualizes the frequency distribution of shot counts across Straight Shot sessions using Recharts.
Table component displaying win/loss records grouped by total ball count for Straight Shot sessions, including the date of first win.
Composed chart component that renders a candlestick-style bar chart (open/close/high/low per day) overlaid with an average trend line for Straight Shot session shot counts.
frontend/src/dev/straight-shot-spot-chec: Manual QA checklist for verifying Straight Shot game functionality remains intact after unrelated code changes, covering session start, shot entry, save, and history display.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: React hook that creates and caches an ICP backend actor using React Query, supporting both anonymous and authenticated (Internet Identity) actors, and invalidates dependent queries when the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useActorRetry.ts: Hook that exposes a `retryConnection` callback to clear and refetch the cached actor query without a page refresh.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides an `InternetIdentityProvider` context and `useInternetIdentity` hook for managing Internet Identity authentication state, including login, logout, and identity persistence via AuthClient.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Centralizes all React Query hooks for backend interactions, including CRUD operations for matches, user profiles, and APA/game-related data using the actor from `useActor`.
frontend/src/index.css: Global CSS file that sets up Tailwind directives and defines CSS custom properties for light and dark theme design tokens (colors, radius, sidebar, charts).
frontend/src/lib/accepting-gifts/accepti: Defines the 12-level Accepting Gifts progression system with level metadata, and provides helpers for clamping indices, retrieving levels, computing max attempts, and calculating the next baseline level after a match.
Manages Accepting Gifts in-session game state, providing pure functions to apply attempt results and prepare the next set within a race-to-7 match.
frontend/src/lib/apa/apaAggregateStats.t: Extracts and aggregates per-player APA match statistics (PPI, aPPI, points, defensive shots) from both practice and official APA match logs, with independent null-safe field cleaning.
frontend/src/lib/apa/apaEqualizer.ts: Defines the APA 9-Ball Equalizer skill level constants and points-to-win mapping, with utility functions for validation and formatting.
frontend/src/lib/apa/apaMatchPoints.ts: Defines the APA 9-Ball 20-point match point conversion chart keyed by loser's skill level (SL1–SL9), and exports functions to calculate and parse match point outcomes based on loser's points pocketed.
frontend/src/lib/apa/apaPracticeMatchOut: Provides a helper function that computes the APA Practice match outcome (winner, match points for each player) given both players' scores, skill levels, and point targets.
frontend/src/lib/apa/apaScoring.ts: Defines APA 9-Ball rack scoring constants and utilities including rack total validation, ball value lookup, PPI calculation, and per-player stats aggregation across racks.
frontend/src/lib/apa/apaSkillLevelPredic: Exports functions that predict an APA skill level (1–9) from PPI or aPPI values using multiple sets of range tables for both the Official APA tab and the Detailed Player Stats page.
frontend/src/lib/apa/officialApaExpected: Contains the official APA Expected PPI lookup table indexed by win-percentage bucket and skill level (SL2–SL7), with helpers to bucket a win percentage and look up the expected PPI value.
Derives an Official APA match outcome ('win', 'loss', or 'unknown') from skill levels and scores by comparing each player's score against their points-to-win target, with a fallback to a saved didWin field.
frontend/src/lib/apa/officialApaPpi.ts: Computes Official APA PPI (score divided by adjusted innings) and aPPI (max of PPI and table-expected PPI for wins only) for a given match, using historical match data to derive the player's win percentage context.
frontend/src/lib/history/matchHistoryRow: Builds structured match result narratives (modeName + details string) for the match history table, handling Official APA, Practice APA, Straight Shots, and Accepting Gifts match modes.
frontend/src/lib/matches/effectiveMatchD: Provides utilities to determine and format the effective date of a match, preferring the user-entered date for Official APA logs and falling back to the stored dateTime timestamp.
frontend/src/lib/matches/matchBuilders.t: Exports a builder for Official APA match log records (including win/loss derivation) and re-exports builders for APA nine-ball, Accepting Gifts, and Straight Shot matches from the original builders file.
frontend/src/lib/matches/matchBuildersOr: Contains builder functions that construct typed MatchLogRecord objects for APA nine-ball practice, Accepting Gifts, and Straight Shot match modes, including UUID generation and outcome computation.
frontend/src/lib/session/inProgressSessi: Provides centralized helpers (get, set, clear, check) for managing per-mode in-progress game state stored in sessionStorage, with typed keys for APA Practice, Accepting Gifts, and Straight Shot modes.
frontend/src/lib/stats/acceptingGiftsPer: Computes win/loss performance statistics for Accepting Gifts matches grouped by starting ball count (2–7), derived from final set scores.
frontend/src/lib/stats/acceptingGiftsSta: Computes summary statistics for Accepting Gifts matches, including total sessions and a rolling average of ending object ball counts over the last N sessions.
Computes aggregate statistics for Official APA match logs, including win rate, last-10 PPI average, and best-10-of-20 aPPI average, sorted chronologically.
frontend/src/lib/stats/straightShotStats: Computes comprehensive Straight Shot statistics including rolling/lifetime averages, lowest shot count, trend data, histogram data, and win/loss summary rows.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that mounts the React app with React Query and Internet Identity providers wrapping the root App component.
frontend/src/pages/accepting-gifts/Accep: The active game page for the Accepting Gifts drill that loads session state from sessionStorage, handles attempt inputs, tracks set scores, and saves completed matches to the backend.
A static card component that displays the rules, level system, match format, and progression rules for the Accepting Gifts drill.

The setup page for Accepting Gifts that loads the player's saved level index and profile, allows selecting a starting level, and initializes a new session in sessionStorage before navigating to the game page.
The APA 9-Ball practice match game page that manages rack-by-rack scoring, tracks cumulative scores for both players, handles seat-to-player remapping based on lag winner, and saves completed matches.
frontend/src/pages/apa/PracticeStartPage: Page for configuring and starting an APA 9-Ball practice match, allowing players to enter names and skill levels, handling existing session detection, lag winner selection, and navigation to the game page.
frontend/src/pages/apa/RealApaMatchEditP: Page for editing an existing official APA match log, loading match data by ID and pre-populating the RealApaMatchForm with existing values.
frontend/src/pages/apa/RealApaMatchPlace: Page for manually logging an official APA match after the fact using the RealApaMatchForm in create mode.
frontend/src/pages/auth/AccessNotAllowed: Page shown to users who lack access, allowing them to submit an access request to the app owner via a button that triggers an approval mutation.
frontend/src/pages/history/MatchDetailsP: Page displaying detailed statistics and metadata for a single match, rendering mode-specific details (APA practice or official) including player stats, PPI, and navigation to player aggregates.
frontend/src/pages/history/MatchHistoryP: Page listing all match history with tabs for filtering by match type (APA, gifts, straight), toggleable sort order, and switchable card/table view modes persisted to localStorage.
frontend/src/pages/HomePage.tsx: The main landing page displaying four mode cards (Official APA Match Log, APA Practice, Accepting Gifts, Straight Shot) and a conditional link to Match History for authenticated users.
frontend/src/pages/owner/OwnerApprovalsP: Admin-only page for managing user access approvals, supporting approve/reject actions on individual or all pending users and toggling invite-only mode.
frontend/src/pages/players/PlayerAggrega: Displays aggregate APA stats for a specific player, including win rate, PPI/aPPI skill level predictions based on best 10 of last 20 official matches, and a matchup analysis dropdown.
frontend/src/pages/SmokeCheckPage.tsx: A post-deployment verification page with a checklist and navigation buttons to Home and Match History for confirming core routing works correctly.
frontend/src/pages/stats/StatsPage.tsx: Main stats dashboard for the logged-in user, showing APA aggregate charts, straight shot trends, accepting gifts performance, and matchup analysis across tabbed sections.
frontend/src/pages/straight-shot/Straigh: Active game page for the Straight Shot drill that loads session state from sessionStorage, lets the user record total shots, and saves the completed match to the backend.
Static card component that displays the rules and objective of the Straight Shot Strokes Drill.
Start page for the Straight Shot drill that shows the player's moving average from recent sessions and lets them start a new session or resume an in-progress one.
frontend/src/utils/errorText.ts: Utility function that extracts a human-readable error string from unknown error types, handling strings, Error objects, and nested object structures.
frontend/src/utils/playerName.ts: Utility functions for normalizing and comparing player names case-insensitively.
frontend/src/utils/playerRoutes.ts: Utility function that builds the URL route path for a player's aggregate stats page using URL encoding.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility functions for reading, storing, and clearing URL parameters from both query strings and hash-based routing, with sessionStorage persistence support.
frontend/src/utils/withTimeout.ts: Exports a `withTimeout` utility that races a given Promise against a setTimeout-based rejection, throwing a configurable error message if the timeout elapses first.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration enabling dark mode, custom OKLCH-based color tokens, typography/container-queries/animate plugins, and accordion keyframe animations.
project_state.json: Tracks the current project state (version 13) for an ICP/Motoko APA Pool Scorer app, documenting architectural notes, known deployment issues, and existing feature implementations including a debug panel and footer toggle.
